page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, configured with 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this server features a shared CPU allocation model with 1.0 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 1280 GB local NVMe SSD, and the system includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

In benchmark testing, the server exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2933.77, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. Multi-core CPU performance is average, yielding a stress-ng multi-core score of 23239.79 and a PassMark CPU score of 18130.87. Memory performance is a notable strength, with a PassMark memory score of 2766.09 ranking in the top 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks show read speeds of 127.29 GB/sec and write speeds of 75.3 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are moderate, with Redis SET operations reaching 15.7 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 8.62 GB/sec.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable CPU performance, though the 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio offers high memory density. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the combination of a 1280 GB NVMe SSD and 64.0 GB of RAM provides a balanced resource profile for memory-intensive and storage-reliant workloads without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. This server is suited for memory-optimized databases, caching layers, web serving, and general-purpose business applications that benefit from high memory capacity and fast local storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 8 physical cores on an AMD EPYC-Rome processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, representing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on a shared CPU allocation model, the server delivers strong single-core CPU performance and strong memory performance, both ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks. Multi-core CPU capabilities and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier, with Redis SET operations achieving 15.7 million operations per second. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio and substantial local NVMe storage, this instance is well-suited for memory-intensive databases, caching systems, and web serving applications that require a balance of memory capacity and fast local storage.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1280s-amd is an 8-vCPU AMD EPYC server with 64 GB RAM and 1280 GB NVMe SSD, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server offering high memory density and fast NVMe storage for database and caching workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
1280 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
